item.title: China Hands Suspended Death Sentences to Former Defense Ministers for Corruption
item.description: The pair were among dozens of senior military figures ousted in the most extensive purges since the Mao era.

item.title: I’m Leaving China After 8 Years. Suspicion of Outsiders Is Rising.
item.description: Our Journal correspondent shares her experience as a Japanese woman reporting for an American newspaper in China.

item.title: U.K. Immigration Official Convicted in Landmark China Spying Case
item.description: The official was one of two men found guilty of helping China spy on dissidents in the U.K.

item.title: U.S. and China Pursue Guardrails to Stop AI Rivalry From Spiraling Into Crisis
item.description: Washington and Beijing recognize that powerful AI models could trigger crises neither side is prepared to manage.

item.title: Trial Begins for Man Accused of Operating Secret Chinese Police Station in New York
item.description: Prosecutors allege the defendant, a U.S. citizen, cooperated with Beijing to harass dissidents.

item.title: China Is Still Supplying Drone Factories in Iran, Russia Despite U.S. Sanctions
item.description: Obscure Chinese firms are openly shipping dual-use goods such as engines and batteries, defying American controls.

item.title: Beijing Deploys Long-Threatened Economic Arsenal Against U.S. Pressure
item.description: Plus, has Xi ‘cracked the code’ on how to manage Trump?

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
